items.q: Is it free to create an account?
items.a: Yes, creating an account is completely free.

items.q: What happens when I win an auction?
items.a: You are contacted directly by the seller to arrange transfer of the vehicle.

items.q: What is a reserve price?
items.a: The reserve price is the minimum amount the seller will accept. The auction only closes as sold if the highest bid meets the reserve.

items.q: Are bids legally binding?
items.a: Yes, placed bids are binding under Danish sale of goods law. Read our terms and conditions for details.

items.q: Can I contact the seller?
items.a: Yes, you can send a direct message to the seller through the platform.

This bid is legally binding under Danish Købeloven. By confirming, you commit to purchasing the vehicle at this price if you win the auction.

Next Auction solely facilitates contact between buyer and seller and is not a party to the transaction.

terms.metaDescription: Read the terms and conditions for using the Next Auction platform.
terms.label: Legal
terms.heading: Terms and Conditions

sections.title: About Next Auction
sections.body:
Next Auction is a digital marketplace platform for vehicles. We connect private and commercial car sellers with potential buyers through online auctions.
Next Auction acts as an intermediary and is not itself the owner or seller of the listed vehicles. Next Auction provides the technical platform and is not a party to the agreement between buyer and seller.

sections.title: Bidding
sections.body:
All bids are binding offers. The highest bid at the close of the auction is considered a binding offer to the seller.
The seller contacts the highest bidder directly after the auction closes to arrange the transfer.
No payment takes place via the Next Auction platform. Trade and payment are agreed directly between buyer and seller.

sections.title: Seller's Responsibility
sections.body:
The seller is solely responsible for:
- The content and accuracy of the listing
- Legal ownership of the vehicle
- Accurate description of the vehicle's condition and mileage
Next Auction does not inspect vehicles.

sections.title: Buyer's Responsibility
sections.body:
Buyers are always encouraged to:
- Physically inspect the vehicle before placing a bid
- Obtain an independent technical assessment
- Check the vehicle's history
Purchases are made at the buyer's own risk.

sections.title: User Types
sections.body:
Private users: Max 2 vehicles per year (SKAT rules).
Business users: Requires a valid CVR number and approval by a Next Auction administrator.

sections.title: Prohibited Conduct
sections.body:
The following is not permitted:
- False or misleading information
- Sale of stolen or encumbered vehicles
- Manipulation of the bidding process
- Registration under a false name
Violation results in immediate exclusion from the platform.

sections.title: Next Auction's Liability
sections.body:
Next Auction is solely an intermediary and accepts no liability for:
- The actual condition of the vehicle
- Completion of the transaction
- Disputes between buyer and seller
- Technical errors that may affect bidding

sections.title: GDPR and Data
sections.body: Next Auction processes your personal data in accordance with GDPR and Danish data protection law. Read our privacy policy for details on collection, storage, and your rights.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body:
If you have questions about these terms, please contact us:
E-mail: support@next-auction.dk
Terms last updated: 1 June 2026.

privacy.metaDescription: Read about how Next Auction processes your personal data.
privacy.label: GDPR
privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: We take your privacy seriously. This policy explains in plain language what information we collect, why we collect it, and what rights you have.

sections.title: Data Controller
sections.body:
Next Auction is the data controller for the personal data we collect via the platform.

Contact: support@next-auction.dk

sections.title: What data do we collect?
sections.body:
We collect the following categories of personal data:

- Contact information: name and email address when creating an account
- Profile information: details you provide in your profile
- Transaction data: information about your bids and auctions
- Technical data: IP address, browser and device type for technical operation and security
- Communications: messages sent via our messaging system

sections.title: Purpose and legal basis
sections.body:
We process your data for the following purposes:

- Service delivery (GDPR art. 6(1)(b)): account creation and operation, bidding and auctions
- Security and abuse prevention (GDPR art. 6(1)(f)): protecting the platform from fraud and abuse
- Legal obligations (GDPR art. 6(1)(c)): retention of data required by Danish law
- Marketing (GDPR art. 6(1)(a)): newsletter and updates, only with your explicit consent

sections.title: Retention
sections.body:
We retain your data for as long as your account is active, and for up to 2 years after inactivity.

Technical logs are deleted on an ongoing basis and retained for a maximum of 90 days.

sections.title: Third-party disclosure
sections.body:
We do not share your personal data with third parties without your consent, unless required by law.

We use the following data processors:
- Vercel (hosting) — USA, EU standard contracts
- Neon (database) — EU
- Cloudinary (images) — USA, EU standard contracts
- Resend (transactional emails) — USA, EU standard contracts
- Upstash (caching) — EU

sections.title: Your rights
sections.body:
Under GDPR you have the following rights:

- Right of access: you can at any time request to see the data we hold about you
- Rectification: you can ask us to correct inaccurate data
- Erasure: you can ask us to delete your data
- Restriction: in certain cases you can request restriction of processing
- Data portability: you can receive your data in a machine-readable format
- Objection: you can object to processing based on legitimate interest

Contact us at support@next-auction.dk. We respond within 30 days.

sections.title: Cookies
sections.body:
Next Auction uses cookies for the technical operation of the platform (session, authentication). These cookies are necessary and do not require consent.

We do not use tracking cookies or third-party advertising cookies.

sections.title: Complaints
sections.body:
If you have objections, you can complain to:

Datatilsynet (Danish Data Protection Agency)
Carl Jacobsens Vej 35, 2500 Valby
www.datatilsynet.dk

We encourage you to contact us first so we can resolve any issues directly.

faqPage.metaDescription: Answers to the most frequently asked questions about Next Auction.
faqPage.label: Help and support
faqPage.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faqPage.contactBody: Contact us directly — we respond within 24 hours.

items.q: What is Next Auction?
items.a: A digital platform that connects car buyers and sellers through real-time auctions. Next Auction is not a party to the transaction — we facilitate contact and auctions.

items.q: Is it free to use?
items.a: Yes, creating an account and placing bids is free.

items.q: Is Next Auction responsible for the transaction?
items.a: No. Next Auction is a matchmaking platform. The transaction is made directly between buyer and seller. We are not a party to the agreement.

items.q: How do I create an account?
items.a: Click 'Create Account' in the top menu and choose between a private or business account.

items.q: Are bids legally binding?
items.a: Yes, placed bids are legally binding under Danish sale of goods law.

items.q: What happens when I win an auction?
items.a: The seller contacts you directly to arrange the handover of the vehicle.

items.q: Can I withdraw my bid?
items.a: No. A bid cannot be withdrawn once placed.

items.q: Should I inspect the car before bidding?
items.a: Yes, we always recommend a physical inspection before placing a bid. Purchases are made at your own risk.

items.q: How many cars can I sell?
items.a: Private users: max 2 cars per year (SKAT rules). Business users: unlimited with a valid CVR number.

items.q: Am I responsible for the listing content?
items.a: Yes, the seller is fully responsible for all information in the listing and for legal ownership of the vehicle.

items.q: What if nobody bids?
items.a: The auction closes without a sale. You can create a new auction.

items.q: When does the seller receive payment?
items.a: Payment is agreed directly between buyer and seller. No payment goes through Next Auction.

items.q: What is prohibited on the platform?
items.a: False information, bid manipulation, and selling stolen vehicles are strictly prohibited and will result in permanent exclusion.

items.q: How are disputes resolved?
items.a: Next Auction does not mediate disputes. Buyer and seller must resolve disagreements directly.

items.q: What are SKAT rules for private sellers?
items.a: Private individuals may sell a maximum of 2 vehicles per calendar year without it being considered commercial activity by SKAT.
